blurb: Screen Time provides parental controls and self-managed usage limits with a security model protecting settings from unauthorized modification and usage data from third-party exposure. Screen Time settings are protected by a separate passcode, preventing children from modifying limits. On devices with iOS 13 or later, usage data synchronizes across a family's devices using the Apple Identity Service end-to-end encrypted protocol, with data transiently stored on IDS servers only until read by the destination device. For multi-device iCloud syncing of personal Screen Time, CloudKit end-to-end encryption ensures Apple cannot read usage patterns, limit configurations, or Downtime schedules.
